The post Can soccer unite Bosnia beyond politics? | A Debrief With Jasmin Mujanović And Dženeta Karabegović appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Braw in Foreign Policy on European response to Russia’s shadow fleet
items.summary: On July 8, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw published an article in Foreign Policy discussing how European governments need to respond to Russia arming its shadow fleet. The post Braw in Foreign Policy on European response to Russia’s shadow fleet appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: The USS Constitution Navigates Future
items.summary: For more than two centuries, the USS Constitution has relied on stars, the sea and paper charts to navigate. But as the vessel prepares for nine America 250 events, Old Ironsides is receiving a digital navigation upgrade courtesy of Naval Surface Warfare Center, Philadelphia Division.

items.title: Preserving Japan's Aviation Heritage: Yokota Air Base Returns Historic Aircraft Artifacts
items.summary: The 374th Civil Engineer Squadron at Yokota Air Base, Japan, returned rare, WWII-era Imperial Japanese Army aircraft artifacts to the Japanese government after uncovering them during a base construction project.

items.title: India’s AI playbook: From talent incubator to AI leader
items.summary: India’s AI talent ecosystem is rapidly growing but faces structural gaps. The post India’s AI playbook: From talent incubator to AI leader appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: AI is the next major biodefense tech priority
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is changing how biological threats can be created, detected, and countered. The post AI is the next major biodefense tech priority appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Retired Submariner Commemorates Both Bicentennial, Semiquincentennial in Guam
items.summary: Fifty years after raising the national colors in Guam for America's bicentennial, retired Navy Cmdr. Allan Riggs returned to the island to commemorate the nation's 250th birthday alongside a new generation of submariners.

items.title: America's 250th: Fireworks, Freedom & Festivities
items.summary: From explosive firework displays and vibrant parades to moving naturalization ceremonies, service members, military families and citizens united across the country to celebrate America's historic 250th anniversary. The national events commemorated 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776.

items.title: 621st Contingency Response Wing Responds to Accelerate Earthquake Disaster Relief in Venezuela
items.summary: The 621st Contingency Response Wing deployed a 110-airman contingency response element to Simón Bolívar International Airport in Maiquetía, Vargas, Venezuela, to work with local authorities and other disaster relief teams to safely expand the intake of humanitarian aid by air transport.

items.title: President, Vice President Address Nation in Celebration of America's 250th Birthday
items.summary: President Donald J. Trump addressed the nation during a "Salute to America" celebration on the National Mall to commemorate America's 250th birthday, while Vice President JD Vance made public comments in New York City earlier in the day.

items.title: Vietnam Veteran Receives Purple Heart 57 Years Later
items.summary: After 57 years, a Vietnam veteran received his long-overdue recognition thanks to a dedicated Army Reserve officer.

items.title: Pennsylvania Army National Guard Conducts 'Ghillie Wash' Training Event
items.summary: Pennsylvania National Guard soldiers participated in a ghillie wash training exercise at Fort Indiantown Gap, Pa., in which they were put through intense physical training in ghillie suits, a garment designed to resemble heavy foliage, grass or terrain.

items.title: Hegseth Thanks National Guard for D.C. Mission Ahead of Nation's 250th
items.summary: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised National Guardsmen in Washington for reducing city crime ahead of America's 250th birthday celebration.

items.title: Build Freedom Initiative Launches With Big Contract to Skilled Trades Champion
items.summary: Michael Duffey, undersecretary of war for acquisition and sustainment, and Michael Cadenazzi, assistant secretary of war for industrial base policy, presented television host Mike Rowe with a $10 million check at the Pentagon to formally launch the Build Freedom Initiative.

items.title: U.S. Participates in Fifth Iteration of Chilean-Hosted Exercise
items.summary: Aircraft, crews and support personnel from across the Americas converged at Cerro Moreno Air Base in Antofagasta, Chile, for the kickoff of Exercise Resolute Sentinel - Salitre 2026.

items.title: U.S. Military Supports Venezuela Earthquake Response
items.summary: U.S. service members are on the ground in Venezuela, delivering assistance where it is needed most following two devastating earthquakes June 24.

items.title: JIATF 401 Leaders Visit Whiteman Air Force Base to Strengthen Counter-Drone Operations
items.summary: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 leaders visited Whiteman Air Force Base, Mo., to learn about the installation's counter-unmanned aerial systems operations and initiatives in advance of the upcoming directed energy pilot program.

items.title: FIIA takes part in European Studies Day 11 May
items.summary: The Centre for European Studies at the University of Helsinki hosts a European Studies Day on 11 May 2026. The event brings together researchers of Europe and the EU from various academic disciplines to present recent research in the field. items.title: Deputy Director Samu Paukkunen will move to a new role starting 1 June
items.summary: Samu Paukkunen, Deputy Director of the Finnish Institute of International Affairs, has resigned from his position and will move to the energy company Fortum as of 1 June 2026. Paukkunen will take up the role of Director, geopolitics and resilience at Fortum. The vacant position will be opened for public application at a later date. […]

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
items.summary: Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: How to implement a full maritime services ban on Russian oil
items.summary: The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price. The ban would cover transportation, insurance, financing, […]
